arts.ttl: Newcastle star ‘excited’ by interest in him – Host of clubs lining up for major deal
arts.sum: Newcastle United star Alexander Isak is ‘excited’ that Barcelona are interested in him, but will have Arsenal, Liverpool and Manchester United to choose from too. That’s according to Mundo Deportivo, who cover the ‘fight’ for the Newcastle striker that is going to take place this summer. They say that Isak is on Barcelona’s agenda as The post Newcastle star ‘excited’ by interest in him – Host of clubs lining up for major deal appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United learn £308m cost as Peter Silverstone PSR issues crystalised by Liverpool result
arts.sum: Newcastle United's challenges around PSR have been made even clear by the news that rivals Liverpool have recorded record commercial revenues in their latest set of accounts. Newcastle's last recorded commercial revenue (for the 2022/23 season) was £46million.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United offered £60m Liverpool flop in 'wild' deal as forgotten man eyes call-up
arts.sum: Newcastle United are on a list of clubs that are set to be offered Liverpool striker Darwin Nunez this summer. The Uruguay international is set to leave Liverpool this summer after being publicly criticised by Arne Slot after a horror miss away to Aston Villa recently.

arts.ttl: Sandro Tonali breaks silence on Newcastle United future after constant AC Milan links
arts.sum: Newcastle United star Sandro Tonali has broken his silence on transfer talk linking him with a move back to Serie A. The Rossoneri have been linked with a move for the star in the last couple of windows while Juventus also made enquiries on the Newcastle midfield ace.

arts.ttl: 10 players could miss Newcastle United vs Brighton through injury after Eddie Howe update
arts.sum: Alexander Isak missed the defeat to Liverpool due to a groin injury but could return to the squad for the clash against Brighton. However, Howe will be hoping that some key members of his squad are available after missing the defeat to Liverpool.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United players set challenge by Eddie Howe
arts.sum: Ahead of the clash against Brighton & Hove Albion, with an FA Cup quarter-final place up for grabs, Howe told Chronicle Live: "We're pleased but we are always pushing and always wanting more. With the trip to Wembley for the Carabao Cup final already secured against Liverpool later this month, Newcastle are also fighting for a place in either the Champions League or Europa League going into the last 11 matches.

arts.ttl: Brighton at Newcastle starting XI predicted as Yankuba Minteh and Danny Welbeck decisions made
arts.sum: All the latest team news for Brighton and Hove Albion ahead of Sunday’s FA Cup fifth round trip to Newcastle

arts.ttl: Alexander Isak Newcastle gamble as Arsenal and Liverpool hear 'special' message
arts.sum: Brighton boss Fabian Hurzeler says FA Cup opponents Newcastle can not replace “special” Alexander Isak should the Swedish striker miss their fifth-round tie through injury. The superstar striker is unlikely to be fit for the clash but the Seagulls chief admits he won't 'gamble' on the player missing the game.

arts.ttl: Brighton transfer news: £7m target shares "plan" to move abroad after 'disappointing' January collapse
arts.sum: Brondby starlet Clement Bischoff expresses his disappointment following the collapse of his January transfer to Brighton and hopes he can move abroad in the near future.

arts.ttl: Is there VAR and extra time in Newcastle United vs Brighton? FA Cup fifth round rules explained
arts.sum: Yes, the fifth round of the competition will see the introduction of VAR after the technology was not in use for previous rounds. It has been confirmed that as well as VAR, the fifth round will see the introduction of semi-automated offsides.
